sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple dots are at:
- (51, 15) - top
- (54, 12) - middle left
- (54, 18) - middle right
- (57, 15) - bottom
This is a 4-dot cross pattern on the left panel. The 3x3 grid on the right has tiles at:
- (50, 30) - top center
- (55, 25) - middle left
- (55, 35) - middle right
- (60, 30) - bottom center (RESET BUTTON)
The pattern maps 1:1 except the bottom tile resets. This is puzzling.
Let me think about the slider more carefully. The slider has a handle that moves. What if:
- Moving the slider to the right end "locks in" or "submits" the current pattern
- The slider position represents progress or confirmation
Currently the slider handle is at cols 25-26 (middle-left). The right end of the track is around col 42.
What if I need to:
1. Set the 3 tiles to green (done)
2. Move the slider all the way to the right to submit/confirm
Let me try moving the slider to the right end using LEFT commands (since LEFT moves the slider right).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: 3 tiles green (T-shape), slider at cols 25-26
# Try moving slider all the way to the right to submit

# First check current slider position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
slider_pos = None
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        if n['boundary']:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            if min(rows) < 25:
                slider_pos = (min(cols), max(cols))
                print(f"Slider at cols {slider_pos[0]}-{slider_pos[1]}")

# Move slider to right end (LEFT moves it right)
# Need to estimate how many LEFT moves to get to the end
# Track seems to go from ~col 13 to ~col 42
# Handle is at ~col 25, need to get to ~col 42
# That's about 17 positions, so ~17 LEFT moves

result = action(['LEFT']*20)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
slider_pos = None
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        if n['boundary']:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            if min(rows) < 25:
                slider_pos = (min(cols), max(cols))
                print(f"Slider now at cols {slider_pos[0]}-{slider_pos[1]}")

# Check tiles
tiles = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['boundary']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        cr, cc = (min(rows)+max(rows))//2, (min(cols)+max(cols))//2
        if 49 <= cr <= 61 and 24 <= cc <= 36 and n['pixels'] == 9:
            tiles[(cr,cc)] = n['color']
print("Tiles:", tiles)
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
print("Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
